# Arkiv

> The Web3 database. A universal data layer for Ethereum — queryable, time-scoped, tamper-proof, wallet-owned.

## Documentation

- Getting started: https://docs.arkiv.network/start-here/fundamentals/
- Installation & Hello World: https://docs.arkiv.network/start-here/installation/
- Testnet (Kaolin): https://docs.arkiv.network/start-here/testnet/
- Best practices: https://docs.arkiv.network/typescript-sdk/best-practices/

## TypeScript SDK

Package: `@arkiv-network/sdk` (NOT `arkiv-sdk` — that package is deprecated)

### Imports

```ts
import { createWalletClient, createPublicClient, http } from "@arkiv-network/sdk"
import { kaolin } from "@arkiv-network/sdk/chains"
import { privateKeyToAccount } from "@arkiv-network/sdk/accounts"
import { eq, gt, lt, gte, lte, desc } from "@arkiv-network/sdk/query"
import { jsonToPayload, stringToPayload, payloadToString, ExpirationTime } from "@arkiv-network/sdk/utils"
```

### Clients

- `createWalletClient({ chain, transport, account })` — read/write, requires private key
- `createPublicClient({ chain, transport })` — read-only, safe for frontend

### Writing data

- `walletClient.createEntity({ payload, contentType, attributes, expiresIn })` → `{ entityKey, txHash }`
- `walletClient.updateEntity({ entityKey, payload, contentType, attributes, expiresIn })` — FULL REPLACE, not patch. Omitted attributes are silently removed.
- `walletClient.deleteEntity({ entityKey })` → `{ txHash }`
- `walletClient.extendEntity({ entityKey, expiresIn })` — extend expiration
- `walletClient.changeOwnership({ entityKey, newOwner })` — transfer ownership
- `walletClient.mutateEntities({ creates, updates, deletes, extensions })` — batch operations

### Reading data

- `publicClient.getEntity(entityKey)` — single entity by key
- `publicClient.buildQuery()` — chainable query builder:
  - `.where(condition)` — filter (eq, gt, lt, gte, lte)
  - `.ownedBy(address)` / `.createdBy(address)` — wallet filters
  - `.orderBy(desc('field', 'type'))` — ordering
  - `.withPayload(true)` / `.withAttributes(true)` — include data
  - `.limit(n)` — max results (cap: 200)
  - `.fetch()` — execute

### Live events

- `publicClient.subscribeEntityEvents({ onEntityCreated, onEntityUpdated, onEntityDeleted, onEntityExpired, onEntityExpiresInExtended, onError }, pollingInterval?, fromBlock?)` — returns `unsubscribe()` function
- NOTE: There is NO `watchEntities` method. Use `subscribeEntityEvents`.

### Payload helpers

- `jsonToPayload(object)` — JSON to payload
- `stringToPayload(string)` — text to payload
- `payloadToString(payload)` — payload to text

### Expiration helpers

- `ExpirationTime.fromMinutes(n)`
- `ExpirationTime.fromHours(n)`
- `ExpirationTime.fromDays(n)`

### Query operators

- `eq(key, value)` — exact match (strings and numbers)
- `gt(key, value)`, `lt()`, `gte()`, `lte()` — numeric comparisons only
- No `contains()`, `startsWith()`, or full-text search at protocol level

## JSON-RPC API

- Querying: https://docs.arkiv.network/json-rpc/querying-data/
- Mutating: https://docs.arkiv.network/json-rpc/mutating-entities/

## Important conventions

- Always use a project attribute on every entity to namespace your app's data
- `updateEntity` is full-replace — re-send ALL attributes you want to keep
- String attributes support only `eq()`. Use numeric values for range queries.
- All entities expire. Use `ExpirationTime` helpers for human-readable durations.

## Testnet

- Chain: Kaolin (ID: 60138453025)
- RPC: https://kaolin.hoodi.arkiv.network/rpc
- WebSocket: wss://kaolin.hoodi.arkiv.network/rpc/ws
- Explorer: https://explorer.kaolin.hoodi.arkiv.network/
- Faucet: https://kaolin.hoodi.arkiv.network/faucet/
